mapping.a_element: braiding of non-Abelian anyons (braid-group / modular-tensor-category structure realized by Ising conformal blocks)
mapping.b_element: Wilson-loop knot invariants of Chern-Simons theory on S^3 (also braid-group / TQFT observables)

mapping.a_element: Chern-Simons / TQFT as the effective description of the topological phase
mapping.b_element: large-N Chern-Simons gauge theory on S^3

brief_justification: Both papers sit in a common Chern-Simons topological-field-theory lineage - A's non-Abelian anyon braiding (Ising conformal blocks) and B's knot invariants (Wilson loops on S^3) are both braid-group/TQFT observables - but the operative machinery differs: A solves a honeycomb spin model by exact free-fermion reduction in a Z2 gauge field and classifies phases by a spectral (band) Chern number nu governing edge thermal transport, whereas B uses a large-N Chern-Simons/topological-string duality on the resolved conifold and BPS (M2/M5-brane) integrality to reformulate knot invariants. The spectral Chern number, the conifold geometry, and the branes have no role-for-role counterparts, so the shared Chern-Simons root is background context rather than the same governing computation.
